title: Syzygy Plasmonics has Signed a Six-Year Agreement with Trafigura for SAF Production
content: The United States Embassy in Uruguay celebrated an important milestone in  innovation, sustainability, and international cooperation. The U.S. company Syzygy Plasmonics has signed a six-year agreement with Trafigura to secure the entirety of the production from its first commercial sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) plant, which will be developed in Uruguay. This agreement supports an innovative and scalable technological model. NovaSAF 1, located in Durazno, Uruguay, will be the first plant in the world to convert biogas into SAF, using Uruguayâ€™s renewable energy and biogas from the dairy sector. The first fuel deliveries are expected to begin in 2028. This project positions Uruguay as a hub for energy innovation and reflects the enormous potential of collaboration between U.S. companies and the local productive ecosystem.
author: Analysts Account
